the Pals tab — not via a Personas menu item. Updated in both the Users Guide

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(worker): fix Dockerfile user setup to prevent build failures (#461)

The worker image build was failing because useradd with --uid 1000 could
fail silently when a user with that UID already exists in the base image.
The `|| true` suppressed the error, but then no home directory was created,
causing subsequent mkdir and chmod commands to fail.

- Remove --uid 1000 constraint to let Docker assign available UID
- Remove 2>/dev/null || true from useradd to surface user creation errors
- Split into separate RUN commands for better layer caching and debugging
- Order operations: create user → create dirs → chmod → copy plugins → chown

Fixes: zmeyd4

Co-authored-by: Homespun Bot <homespun@localhost>

commit.message:
Fix remote builder validation to work on all architectures

Kamal's `remote?` method checks if any arches would be built remotely
(arches that don't match the local machine), not whether a remote is
configured. On x86_64 CI with arch: amd64, all arches are local so
`remote?` returns false even with remote: "ssh://host" configured.

Use `config.builder.remote` (the raw config value) instead to reject
remote builders regardless of architecture matching.

commit.message:
security: Harden state validation, auth, and crypto primitives

- compute-economics: Bounded pricing ($0.01-$100/MTok), per-call token
  cap (200K), quality clamping [0,5], state file validation
- consensus: HMAC authentication for inter-shard communication,
  replay protection with 1h expiry, signed broadcast payloads
- mining: State file validation before loading, reject corrupted
  negative balances, graceful fallback to default state
- shadow: Use crypto.randomInt() instead of Math.random() for
  codename generation (cryptographically secure)
- web-api: Log parse failures with IP, fail-closed on auth parse
  errors instead of falling through to body userId

commit.message:
fix: HealthKit integration — race condition + silent data fetch failures

Three issues fixed:

1. Race condition: initHealthKit() yielded at await HealthPlugin.isAvailable(),
   allowing handleRemoteDataUpdate() to replace state.settings mid-check.
   The remote merge defaulted healthKitConnected to false, causing the setup
   modal to reappear on every launch. Fixed by capturing the local value
   before the await, and preserving local healthKitConnected during merge
   (it is device-specific, not a synced setting).

2. queryAggregated for heartRateVariability always rejected — the plugin
   explicitly blocks aggregated queries for HRV. This caused Promise.all
   in refreshHealthKitData() to reject, preventing ALL HealthKit data
   (sleep, heart, steps) from ever displaying. Fixed by using readSamples
   for HRV with manual daily averaging.

3. Promise.all to Promise.allSettled in refreshHealthKitData() so one
   failing query does not cascade-kill the others.

Also: await saveSample in sleep write, log denied auth types, add
healthKitConnected to backward compat defaults in loadState().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add deploy-succeeded plugin to auto-purge CDN cache

Local Netlify plugin at netlify/plugins/cache-purge that runs onSuccess
(after deploy is live). Calls POST /api/v1/purge to flush the entire
CDN edge cache so stale HTML with old chunk hashes is never served.

Requires NETLIFY_AUTH_TOKEN env var set in Netlify dashboard.
Fails gracefully if token is missing — won't break deploys.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: edit_live を false に変更（配信準備完了状態に対応） (#145)

ライブバージョンの編集リソースが見つからずタイムアウトする。
edit_live: false で準備中バージョンにメタデータを適用する。

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix base schedule save blocking own shift regeneration via shift_blocks

When saving a base schedule, autoApplyBaseSchedules removes old auto-shifts
(with skipBlock=true) then regenerates new ones. But pre-existing shift_blocks
from previous manual deletions would prevent new shifts from being created,
causing the employee to disappear from the planning view.

Fix: add clearBlocks option to autoApplyBaseSchedules. When saving a base
schedule, clearBlocks=true clears all shift_blocks in the date range before
regenerating. Regular page-load regeneration still respects blocks.

Also adds DELETE /shift-blocks/range endpoint for bulk block deletion.

commit.message:
Handle TLS authentication challenges to fix Microsoft device compliance (#806)

WKWebView rejects all authentication challenges by default when
webView(_:didReceive:completionHandler:) is not implemented, using
.rejectProtectionSpace. This silently breaks TLS client-certificate
flows like Microsoft Entra ID Conditional Access, which verifies
device compliance via a certificate stored in the system keychain
by MDM enrollment.

By implementing the delegate method and returning
.performDefaultHandling, the system's standard URL-loading behaviour
takes over: the keychain is searched for matching client identities,
MDM-installed root CAs are trusted, and any configured SSO extensions
(e.g. Microsoft Enterprise SSO) can intercept the challenge.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
refactor: two-pass D1 loader to avoid SQLITE_TOOBIG errors

Rewrote load_to_d1.py to split metadata UPSERT (11 batches, ~850KB)
from JSON blob UPDATE (484 batches, ~750KB). Original single-pass
embedded JSON blobs inline causing 51/53 batches to exceed D1's ~1MB
statement size limit. Added --meta-only flag for quick metadata updates.

commit.message: v0.4.4 – Heuristic fallback for BEM-named author blocks Adds a broad XPath fallback in extract_author_context() that fires when specific class patterns fail. Detects author bio cards containing an and >100 chars of text (e.g. BEM names like issue-article__author), then extracts bio text and photo URL using the same sub-element priorities as the named-class path. The same heuristic is added to extract_body_html() so the matched block is stripped from post content. Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6

commit.message:
Fix React render error: Objects not valid as React child

Guard all rendering of Roanuz API fields against non-string/non-number
objects. Root cause: messages[0].value from Roanuz can be a structured
object {innings, over_number} rather than a string, causing statusNote
to be an object that React cannot render as a child.

- full-live/route.ts: statusNote now always returns a string (typeof guard)
- page.tsx Scorecard: inn.runRate rendered only when typeof === 'number'
- page.tsx Scorecard: inn.scoreStr guarded with typeof === 'string'
- page.tsx Scorecard: match.statusNote render guarded with typeof === 'string'
